issues.title: Blob URL not cleared from state when file is removed externally
issues.description: When `file` becomes null (external clear), the effect at line 43 calls `URL.revokeObjectURL(previewUrlRef.current)` but does NOT call `setPreviewUrl(null)`. While the component comment argues the stale state never renders because `file` being null causes the upload button to render instead of the img, this creates an internal state inconsistency: `previewUrl` state still holds a now-revoked URL. On the next re-render cycle if `file` transitions back quickly (fast file replacement), `previewUrl` could be briefly non-null and pointing to a revoked URL, causing a broken image flash. The ref cleanup path and state cleanup path are diverged.

issues.title: new Date() called on potentially invalid/empty created_at string
issues.description: In `buildOrderRow`, `new Date(order.created_at).toLocaleString()` will produce the string `'Invalid Date'` if `order.created_at` is an empty string or a malformed ISO string. The domain type `SellerReportOrder.created_at` is typed as `string` (not `string | null`), but if the API returns an unexpected value or empty string, the cell will contain 'Invalid Date' in the exported file without any warning. There is no guard or fallback.
issues.fix:
Add a guard before constructing the Date:
```ts
const dateStr = order.created_at
  ? new Date(order.created_at).toLocaleString()
  : '';
toCell(dateStr)
```

issues.title: Second useEffect can inadvertently reset the completed checkout flag
issues.description: At line 113, the effect fires whenever `groups.length` or `hasCompletedCheckout` changes. The condition `groups.length > 0 && hasCompletedCheckout && !cartCleared.current` was intended to reset the flag if the user navigated away and came back with a non-empty cart. However, `cartCleared.current` is a ref that persists only for the current component lifetime. If the component unmounts and remounts (e.g., navigating away and back), `cartCleared.current` is reset to `false` on the new mount, meaning this effect WILL fire and call `writeCompletedCheckoutFlag(false)` — clearing the session flag — even if the cart cookie was already cleared. This causes the completed checkout screen to not show on re-mount until cart cookie change is detected, creating a race between the cookie being cleared and the component reading the session flag.
issues.fix: Re-evaluate whether this cleanup effect is needed. If intentional, consider adding an additional condition or documentation explaining the lifecycle contract. At minimum, the second effect should be a no-op when `cartCleared.current` is false on re-mount after cookie was already cleared.

issues.title: validateStock returns true (has issues) when product belongs to DIFFERENT seller
issues.description: In `validateStock`, the condition at line 161 returns `true` (stock issue) when `product.seller_id !== sellerId`. This is used as a security guard: if a product doesn't belong to the expected seller, it's treated as a stock issue. However, this silently suppresses the payment methods response (`methods: []`) rather than returning an explicit error. A malicious client constructing an items payload with mixed-seller product IDs would receive `hasStockIssues: true` with an empty methods array — which is the correct outcome (no payment methods disclosed), but the client receives no indication of why. This is by design per the checkout-stock-integrity rule, but worth noting as a potential confusion source for debugging real stock issues vs. mis-matched seller attacks.
issues.fix: This is intentional security behavior per the checkout-stock-integrity rule. Consider adding a server-side log (not visible to clients) distinguishing stock issues from seller mismatch for debugging purposes.

issues.title: No null/undefined guard on insert response array access
issues.description: At line 462, `ev[0].id` accesses the first element of the array returned by `insert('events', EVENT)`. The `insert` function returns `res.json()` which is typed as `any`. If Supabase returns an empty array (e.g., due to RLS rules or misconfiguration), `ev[0]` is `undefined` and `.id` will throw a TypeError. Similarly, `p[0].id` at line 522 and `method[0].id` at line 551 have the same issue.

issues.title: Debounced save may fire after unmount if component unmounts during debounce window
issues.description: The cleanup `useEffect` at line 99 clears the debounce timer on unmount. However, the `mutationRef.current.mutate(...)` call (line 49) uses a stored reference to the mutation object. If the component unmounts during the 500ms debounce window and the cleanup fires AFTER `setTimeout` has already been cleared (race), the saved `mutate` callback could still be called. In practice React's cleanup ordering prevents this, but the `onSuccess` callback at line 51 calls `setSaveStatus('saved')` on a potentially unmounted component. TanStack Query's `useMutation` does not automatically abort on unmount like `useQuery`.

issues.title: Error message extraction falls through to error.message on non-Error objects
issues.description: At lines 88-90, the error message extraction first checks `error instanceof Error`, then uses `(error as { message?: string })?.message ?? t('errorOccurred')`. This is a safe pattern, but the double `.message` access (once via the Error branch and once via the cast) is slightly redundant. The fallback is correctly handled. No runtime bug, just a minor readability note.
issues.fix:
No action required — the pattern is safe. Could be simplified to:
```ts
const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : t('errorOccurred');
```

issues.title: Login URL construction may produce double-slash for relative authHostUrl paths
issues.description: At line 67, when `authHostUrl` starts with `/` (e.g. `/auth`), the `base` is set to `authHostUrl` directly. The `loginUrl` is built as `${globalThis.location.origin}${base}${loginPath}`. If `authHostUrl` is `/auth` and `loginPath` is `/en/login?returnTo=...`, the final URL is `http://origin/auth/en/login?returnTo=...` which is correct. However, if `authHostUrl` does NOT start with `/` and is also not an absolute URL (e.g. `auth`), `base` becomes `/auth` (prepending slash), which is correct. The logic appears safe but the string concatenation approach for URL construction is error-prone compared to using the URL constructor. This is informational.
issues.fix: Consider using `new URL()` constructor for URL assembly to avoid potential double-slash or malformed URL edge cases.

issues.title: currencies memo filters with Boolean but currency is string - falsy empty string handled correctly
issues.description: At line 24, `.filter(Boolean)` on `orders.map(o => o.currency)` correctly removes any null/undefined/empty string currencies before deduplication. The `SellerReportOrder` type defines `currency` as `string` (not nullable), so this filter is a defensive guard. No bug — this is safe and correct.
